content:
# Judges Chapter 9 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), c. 1008 CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/JDG_page_277.jpg, JDG_page_278.jpg, JDG_page_279.jpg

1. And Avimelekh son of Yerubba'al went to Sh'khem, to his mother's brothers, and he spoke to them and to all the clan of the house of his mother's father, saying,

2. "Speak, I pray, in the ears of all the lords of Sh'khem: 'Which [is] better for you — that seventy men rule over you, all the sons of Yerubba'al, or that one man rule over you?' And remember that I [am] your bone and your flesh."

3. And his mother's brothers spoke of him in the ears of all the lords of Sh'khem all these words. And their heart inclined after Avimelekh, for they said, "He [is] our brother."

4. And they gave him seventy [pieces] of silver from the house of Ba'al-B'rit. And Avimelekh hired with them worthless and reckless men, and they followed him.

5. And he went to his father's house at Ophrah and killed his brothers, the sons of Yerubba'al — seventy men — on one stone. But Yotam, the youngest son of Yerubba'al, was left, for he hid himself.

6. And all the lords of Sh'khem and all Beit-Millo gathered together, and they went and made Avimelekh king, by the oak of the pillar which [was] in Sh'khem.

7. And they told Yotam, and he went and stood on the top of Mount G'rizim, and he lifted his voice and called out and said to them, "Listen to me, lords of Sh'khem, and may God listen to you.

8. The trees went, going, to anoint over them a king. And they said to the olive tree, 'Reign over us.'

9. And the olive tree said to them, 'Shall I cease my fatness, by which gods and men are honored through me, and go to sway over the trees?'

10. And the trees said to the fig tree, 'You — come, reign over us.'

11. And the fig tree said to them, 'Shall I cease my sweetness and my good fruit, and go to sway over the trees?'

12. And the trees said to the vine, 'You — come, reign over us.'

13. And the vine said to them, 'Shall I cease my new wine, which gladdens gods and men, and go to sway over the trees?'

14. And all the trees said to the thornbush, 'You — come, reign over us.'

15. And the thornbush said to the trees, 'If in truth you are anointing me king over you, come, take refuge in my shade. And if not, let fire come out from the thornbush and devour the cedars of the L'vanon.'

16. And now, if in truth and in integrity you have acted, and you have made Avimelekh king, and if you have dealt well with Yerubba'al and with his house, and if according to the dealing of his hands you have done to him —

17. for my father fought for you and risked his life and delivered you from the hand of Midyan,

18. and you have risen against my father's house today and have killed his sons — seventy men — on one stone, and you have made Avimelekh, the son of his maidservant, king over the lords of Sh'khem, because he [is] your brother —

19. if in truth and in integrity you have dealt with Yerubba'al and with his house this day, then rejoice in Avimelekh, and let him also rejoice in you.

20. And if not, let fire come out from Avimelekh and devour the lords of Sh'khem and Beit-Millo, and let fire come out from the lords of Sh'khem and from Beit-Millo and devour Avimelekh."

21. And Yotam fled and ran away, and he went to Be'er and dwelt there, because of Avimelekh his brother.

22. And Avimelekh ruled over Yisrael three years.

23. And God sent an evil spirit between Avimelekh and the lords of Sh'khem, and the lords of Sh'khem dealt treacherously with Avimelekh,

24. that the violence [done] to the seventy sons of Yerubba'al might come, and their blood be placed upon Avimelekh their brother who killed them, and upon the lords of Sh'khem who strengthened his hands to kill his brothers.

25. And the lords of Sh'khem set ambushers against him on the tops of the mountains, and they robbed everyone who passed by them on the road. And it was told to Avimelekh.

26. And Ga'al son of Eved came with his brothers and crossed over to Sh'khem. And the lords of Sh'khem put their trust in him.

27. And they went out into the field and gathered their vineyards and trod [them] and held a festival. And they went into the house of their god and ate and drank and cursed Avimelekh.

28. And Ga'al son of Eved said, "Who [is] Avimelekh, and who [is] Sh'khem, that we should serve him? [Is he] not the son of Yerubba'al? And Z'vul [is] his officer. Serve the men of Chamor, the father of Sh'khem! And why should we serve him?

29. And would that this people were in my hand! Then I would remove Avimelekh." And he said to Avimelekh, "Increase your army and come out!"

30. And Z'vul the ruler of the city heard the words of Ga'al son of Eved, and his anger burned.

31. And he sent messengers to Avimelekh in Tormah, saying, "Behold, Ga'al son of Eved and his brothers are coming to Sh'khem, and behold, they are stirring up the city against you.

32. And now, arise by night — you and the people who [are] with you — and lie in wait in the field.

33. And it shall be in the morning, as the sun rises, you shall rise early and rush upon the city. And behold, he and the people who [are] with him come out against you, and you shall do to him as your hand finds."

34. And Avimelekh and all the people who [were] with him arose by night, and they lay in wait against Sh'khem — four companies.

35. And Ga'al son of Eved went out and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city. And Avimelekh and the people who [were] with him arose from the ambush.

36. And Ga'al saw the people and said to Z'vul, "Behold, people are coming down from the tops of the mountains." And Z'vul said to him, "The shadow of the mountains you are seeing as men."

37. And Ga'al spoke again and said, "Behold, people are coming down from the navel of the land, and one company is coming from the way of the Oak of the Diviners."

38. And Z'vul said to him, "Where now [is] your mouth, you who said, 'Who [is] Avimelekh that we should serve him?' Is this not the people whom you despised? Go out now and fight with him."

39. And Ga'al went out before the lords of Sh'khem and fought with Avimelekh.

40. And Avimelekh chased him, and he fled before him. And many fell slain, up to the entrance of the gate.

41. And Avimelekh dwelt in Arumah. And Z'vul drove out Ga'al and his brothers from dwelling in Sh'khem.

42. And it was the next day, the people went out to the field, and they told Avimelekh.

43. And he took the people and divided them into three companies and lay in wait in the field. And he looked, and behold, the people were coming out of the city. And he arose against them and struck them.

44. And Avimelekh and the companies that [were] with him rushed and stood at the entrance of the gate of the city. And two companies rushed upon all who [were] in the field and struck them.

45. And Avimelekh fought against the city all that day and captured the city. And the people who [were] in it he killed, and he tore down the city and sowed it with salt.

46. And all the lords of the Tower of Sh'khem heard, and they entered the stronghold of the house of El-B'rit.

47. And it was told to Avimelekh that all the lords of the Tower of Sh'khem had gathered together.

48. And Avimelekh went up to Mount Tsalmon — he and all the people who [were] with him. And Avimelekh took the axes in his hand and cut a branch from the trees. And he lifted it and put it on his shoulder and said to the people who [were] with him, "What you have seen me do — hurry, do as I [have done]."

49. And all the people also cut, each his branch, and they went after Avimelekh and put [them] against the stronghold and set the stronghold on fire over them. And all the men of the Tower of Sh'khem also died — about a thousand men and women.

50. And Avimelekh went to Tevets and encamped against Tevets and captured it.

51. And there was a strong tower in the middle of the city, and all the men and women and all the lords of the city fled there. And they shut [it] behind them and went up on the roof of the tower.

52. And Avimelekh came to the tower and fought against it. And he drew near to the door of the tower to burn it with fire.

53. And a certain woman threw an upper millstone upon the head of Avimelekh and crushed his skull.

54. And he called quickly to the young man bearing his armor and said to him, "Draw your sword and kill me, lest they say of me, 'A woman killed him.'" And his young man thrust him through, and he died.

55. And the men of Yisrael saw that Avimelekh was dead, and they went, each to his place.

56. And God returned the evil of Avimelekh, which he did to his father, to kill his seventy brothers.

57. And all the evil of the men of Sh'khem, God returned upon their head. And the curse of Yotam son of Yerubba'al came upon them.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.4 — "worthless and reckless men":** anashim reqim uphochazim — literally "empty and reckless men." reqim ("empty") describes moral emptiness; pochazim ("reckless/bubbling over") describes dangerous impulsivity.

2. **v.8-15 — Jotham's Fable:** The only true fable in the Hebrew Bible — trees seeking a king. The olive, fig, and vine each refuse because they have productive work to do. Only the thornbush (atad), which produces nothing, accepts. The fable's point: only the worthless seek power.

3. **v.37 — "the navel of the land":** tabbur ha'arets — literally "the navel of the earth/land." A geographic term for the center of the land, possibly a sacred hilltop.

4. **v.53 — "an upper millstone":** pelach rekhev — the upper stone of a hand mill, a heavy domestic object. Abimelech, who killed seventy brothers on "one stone" (v.5), is killed by one stone dropped by one woman.

— EMET, AI Translator
